{"step_by_step_thinking": "Enlarged adenoids lead to nasal obstruction, which results in mouth breathing. Chronic mouth breathing is associated with craniofacial abnormalities, including a narrow maxillary arch and a high-arched palate. Multiple documents describe 'adenoid facies' as a characteristic facial appearance in children with adenoid hypertrophy, which includes a narrow upper alveolus, narrowed maxillary and dental arches, and a high-arched palate. Document [1] explicitly states that adenoid hypertrophy leads to narrowed maxillary and dental arches. Document [14] also notes that chronic mouth breathing due to adenoid hypertrophy leads to midfacial growth abnormalities, including a high-arched palate and retrognathic mandible, which is consistent with a narrowed maxilla. There is no evidence in the documents supporting widening of the maxilla, palatal plane tipping upwards at PNS, or the palate descending. Therefore, the most accurate effect of enlarged adenoids on maxillary growth is narrowing of the maxilla.", "answer_choice": "A"}
